name: Loyal Adkison
motto: "Get money, still get money, boys, No matter by what means."
description: He is a very genial sort of fellow—always enjoying a joke, whether it is a good one or not.

name: Catherine T. Bryden
motto: "She hath a unique affliction—she is called a sensible girl."
description: She is noted among the "naughty fours" because she studies. She always has her lessons. That's what she is coming to College for. She joined our favored ranks when we were Freshmen, but has proved herself a member to be proud of.

name: John Auld
motto: "I must be a very fascinating young man; 'Tis not my fault; the girls must blame heaven."
description: He went to Berkeley a couple of years ago, but decided he liked the old University of Idaho better, after all. He, too, is considered too young (?) to graduate with the '04s, so he doesn't know whether to go to Berkeley and "dig," or to Wardner and "muck;" he has been with the class since its earlier days.

name: Zoia Clark
motto: "Her virtue, and her consciousness of her worth; That would be wooed, and not unsought be won."
description: She has missed a good deal of time at College, but by dint of hard working, has stuck fast to the '04's, from Junior Prep. up; she usually has somebody to carry her books down from the Varsity every afternoon, except during the training season.

name: Mason Hume Cornwall
motto: "Modest doubt is called The beacon of the wise."
description: Another one of the sort rare among '04s, unassuming and studious; he is usually on hand, though, when anything is going on and gives his support; an old '04.

name: Claude Anderson Dunbar
motto: "Villain and he were many miles apart."
description: He is considered by one member of the Faculty "too young to graduate." For the rest let him pass in peace; another member of Prep. '00.

name: Earl, David
motto: "To die and part is a less evil; but to part and live, there, there's the torment."
description: He is noted for his legs, and his fondness for horseback riding. He is usually in everything which is going on, and is on nearly every committee in College; a member when the '04s were '00 Preps. He is one of the most popular fellows in the 'Varsity although some people don't realize it.

name: Edgett, Clarence Mills
motto: "I was not born for courts or great affairs, I pay my debts, believe, and say my prayers."
description: Our famous center; he is now running an "Irishman's Buggy" in the Lady mine at Wallace; we suppose that he is doing as much thinking 3,000 feet below ground as he used to do when above.

name: Fisher, Robert William
motto: "His wit is more than many, his innocence a child."
clubs: the third member of the "D trio"
description: Our man for jokes; he thinks he can make pretty good ones, and sometimes he can; his work needs a good deal of copartnership with the Art Department—or seems to, at any rate; considered too young for the '04s; has ever been a good '04.

name: Fogle, Mary Elinor
motto: "The joy of youth and health her eyes displayed, And ease of heart her every look conveyed."
description: She joined us this year from the ranks of the '05 class, and has the reputation of being one of the best natured girls around College; she's always giggling, and wears a long ulster with her hands in her pockets.

name: Forney, Cora May
motto: "Or if thou thinkst I am too quickly won, I'll frown and say thee nay, so thou wilt woo."
description: The other '04 who is fond of horseback riding; she likes a good time, in any style, but studies, too, when there's nothing better to be done; to a few persons she is known as Clarinda, but not generally; an '04 since its baby days; she has a dog "with a pedigree."

name: Haynes, Fred Albert
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: He came to the University of Idaho for the purpose of getting an education, and incidentally to try to argue everybody out of every idea they ever had, and into every one they hadn't; however, he will learn more in time.

name: Henderson, Margaret
motto:
"Be to her virtues very kind
Be to her faults a little blind."
description: A worthy member of the '04s from its Prep days. She holds her class first, the Monroe doctrine second, and her studies third; she is least but first.

name: Hooper, Clarence M.
motto:
"Anglo-Saxon, Latin and Greek,
All the dead languages he doth seek."
description: He is a student; Greek and Latin are his hobby. But in him the Juniors expect a transformation; for him we would still have hopes if it were not for the fact that his affections were becoming alienated from our class and being transferred to the world of femininity, in which he can as yet see nothing but blissful serenity.

name: Keener, Trula Laura
description: She has been to one class meeting this year; to tell the truth, she seems to find Preps much more interesting than the members of the upper classes. But such is the way of the world.

name: Kirkwood, John Howard
motto: "I have been told by some of my friends that I am too modest."
description: He can work when he wants to, but we won't say how often that is; he is principally noted for his whistle and his tenor voice; since the spring of 1901 he has been known by the name of "Regent;" an '04 from its early days.

name: Martin, Mabyl
motto: "A pleasing countenance is no slight advantage."
description: She has been a good '04 ever since we can remember; she is a tall and stately young lady, and "wears" a rather scornful expression most of the time; but that is misleading, for she doesn't feel that way.

name: Mitchell, Robert Colfax
motto: "Man is not able to invent anything that tends to laughter more than I invent or is invented on me."
clubs: Manager, the Ridenbaugh Hall Base Ball Nine
description: Last year he was adopted as a member of the '04 class, and has since proved to be a rather silent but efficient member; and, it is reported, is interested in the building of a steamboat of his own invention.

name: Jennie, Peterson A. B.
motto: "How doth the little busy bee Improve each shining hour."
description: She studies Latin and Greek most of her time, and when she isn't digging at either of those, then it's something else; she is never seen wasting time in frivolous pleasures.

name: Charles, Dennis Saxton A. B.
motto: "Without being at all unnatural, he has an amazing fund of peculiarity."
description: The other elocutionary genius of the Class of '04; truly an astonishing specimen; his bald head and massive voice are his most remarkable characteristics.

name: Florence, Skattaboe A. B.
motto: "I have no other than a woman's reason: I think him so because I think him so."
description: She is the artist of the Junior Class, and a hustler; she knows how to make other people work for her, too; she is one of the most devoted of our tribe although she only joined us last year.

name: Smith, Harry Clarence
motto: "A solemn youth with sober phiz, Who eats his grub and minds his biz."
description: He is known as "Silent" for various reasons, but can warm up to a point when necessary; he is renowned for his ability to carry anything through which he once undertakes, and has held almost every office in the '04 Class.

name: Wahl, Edward
motto: "I swear he is true hearted and none better in my kingdom."
description: A very modest, unassuming lad, who studies and collects plants; his thirst for the latter occupation seems unquenchable; however, he is learning to dance this year, which shows he is on the high road to recovery.

name: Zeigler, Roy
motto: "So wise so young, they say, do ne'er live long."
clubs: Editor in Chief of "Gem of the Mountains"
description: Look at his picture; that will tell you what he is, better than any eulogy we could give.
